en.title: Process Mining on the Salesforce Sales Funnel with Fluxicon Disco
en.tagline: The funnel on the slide was linear. The funnel in Salesforce had 180+ variants. Disco made that gap impossible to ignore.
en.industry: Energy (Oil & Gas) · national sales organisation, 400+ reps
en.problem: Sales leadership was convinced the opportunity flow followed the textbook: Qualification → Needs Analysis → Proposal → Negotiation → Closed. In reality, only 1 in 6 deals moved through that happy path. Deals jumped backward, skipped validation, stalled between approvers — and the forecast debate every quarter was really a debate about a process nobody had ever actually looked at.
data: Salesforce OpportunityHistory: every stage change with timestamp + user
User object: role, region, line manager — for resource-level analysis
solution: Pulled a clean event log from Salesforce via SOQL + Python (pm4py): case id = OpportunityId, activity = new stage, resource = user. Reproducible as a monthly refresh.
Loaded the log into Fluxicon Disco — 180+ unique paths collapsed into 6 dominant patterns covering 84% of deals.

Used Disco's bottleneck view to locate the real waiting points. The slowest step was not 'Proposal → Negotiation' as everyone assumed — it was 'Qualification → Proposal' (deals waiting on a central pricing team).
Quantified rework loops: deals bouncing between Proposal and Qualification more than once = 22% of cycle-time overrun. Built a Power BI monitor on the same KPIs so findings survive the presentation.

impact.label: Median cycle time
impact.detail: after removing the main rework loop

en.takeaway: You cannot improve a sales process you have never actually looked at. Disco on top of OpportunityHistory takes days, not quarters — and changes the conversation from opinion to evidence.

it.title: Process Mining sul funnel Salesforce con Fluxicon Disco
it.tagline: Il funnel sulla slide era lineare. Il funnel in Salesforce aveva 180+ varianti. Disco ha reso il gap impossibile da ignorare.
it.industry: Energy (Oil & Gas) · sales organisation nazionale, 400+ rep
it.problem: La sales leadership era convinta che le opportunity seguissero il manuale: Qualification → Needs Analysis → Proposal → Negotiation → Closed. In realtà solo 1 deal su 6 seguiva quel percorso. I deal tornavano indietro, saltavano la validation, restavano fermi tra approvatori — e il dibattito sul forecast ogni trimestre era in realtà un dibattito su un processo che nessuno aveva mai guardato davvero.
data: Salesforce OpportunityHistory: ogni cambio stage con timestamp + utente
Oggetto User: ruolo, region, manager di linea — per l'analisi a livello di risorsa
solution: Estratto un event log pulito da Salesforce via SOQL + Python (pm4py): case id = OpportunityId, activity = nuovo stage, resource = utente. Riproducibile come refresh mensile.
Caricato il log su Fluxicon Disco — 180+ percorsi unici collassati in 6 pattern dominanti che coprono l'84% dei deal.

Usata la vista bottleneck di Disco per trovare i veri punti di attesa. Lo step più lento non era 'Proposal → Negotiation' come tutti pensavano — era 'Qualification → Proposal' (deal in attesa del team centrale di pricing).

Quantificati i loop di rilavorazione: deal che rimbalzavano tra Proposal e Qualification più di una volta = 22% dello sforamento di cycle time. Costruito un monitor Power BI sugli stessi KPI perché i fix restino.

impact.label: Varianti comprese
impact.detail: pattern che coprono l'84% dei deal

impact.label: Cycle time mediano
impact.detail: dopo aver tolto il loop principale

it.takeaway: Non puoi migliorare un sales process che non hai mai davvero guardato. Disco sopra OpportunityHistory prende giorni, non trimestri — e sposta la conversazione da opinione a evidenza.
